title: Comparison Operators
description: Compare values and get boolean results.

slides.title: Comparing Values
slides.content:
# Comparison Operators

These operators compare two values and return a boolean (`true` or `false`).

*   `==`  Equal (value only)
*   `===` Identical (value and type)
*   `!=`  Not equal
*   `<>`  Not equal (same as `!=`)
*   `!==` Not identical
*   `<`   Less than
*   `>`   Greater than
*   `<=`  Less than or equal to
*   `>=`  Greater than or equal to
*   `<=>` Spaceship (PHP 7+) – returns -1, 0, or 1

slides.title: Equal vs Identical
slides.content:
```php
<?php
$a = 5;      // integer
$b = "5";    // string

var_dump($a == $b);   // bool(true)  – value equal after type juggling
var_dump($a === $b);  // bool(false) – different types

var_dump($a != $b);   // bool(false) – not equal? Wait, they are equal in value, so false
var_dump($a !== $b);  // bool(true)  – they are not identical
?>
```

slides.title: Inequality Examples
slides.content:
```php
<?php
$x = 10;
$y = 20;

var_dump($x < $y);   // bool(true)
var_dump($x > $y);   // bool(false)
var_dump($x <= 10);  // bool(true)
var_dump($y >= 20);  // bool(true)

// Spaceship operator
var_dump($x <=> $y); // int(-1) because $x < $y
var_dump($y <=> $x); // int(1)  because $y > $x
var_dump($x <=> 10); // int(0)  because equal
?>
```
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
$x = 10;
$y = 20;

var_dump($x < $y);
var_dump($x > $y);
var_dump($x <= 10);
var_dump($y >= 20);
var_dump($x <=> $y);
var_dump($y <=> $x);
var_dump($x <=> 10);
?>

quizData.question: The expression `0 == false` is true.
quizData.explanation: Yes, because `false` is converted to 0 for comparison, so they are equal.

quizData.question: What is the result of `1 === true`?
quizData.explanation: `===` checks type and value. 1 is integer, true is boolean, so they are not identical.
